- Block at (35, 23): part of frame 1 cycle (position 7) and frame 3 cycle (position 3)

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try rotating frame 2 through all positions and checking for level completion
# First, let me save the current state

seg = current_frame.segmentation
blocks_before = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 4:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))//2, (min(cols)+max(cols))//2)
        blocks_before[center] = n['color']

# Try rotating frame 2 (left button at 26,14) through all 8 positions
for i in range(8):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 26, 'col': 14}])
    
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done') or last_action_result.get('run_complete'):
        print(f"Level completed after {i+1} rotations of frame 2!")
        break
    
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    blocks_after = {}
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['pixels'] == 4:
            boundary = n['boundary']
            rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
            cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
            center = ((min(rows)+max(rows))//2, (min(cols)+max(cols))//2)
            blocks_after[center] = n['color']
    
    # Check if frame 2 has a notable pattern
    f2_cycle = [(26,17), (26,23), (26,26), (26,29), (26,32), (26,35), (26,38), (26,44)]
    f2_vals = [blocks_after[p] for p in f2_cycle]
    
    # Check opposite pairs
    opp_matches = sum(1 for j in range(4) if f2_vals[j] == f2_vals[j+4])
    
    # Check if any special pattern
    print(f"f2 rot {i+1}: {f2_vals} | opp_matches={opp_matches}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

f2 rot 8: ['g', 'w', 'b', 'b', 'w', 'S', 'p', 'S'] | opp_matches=0
